senses.gloss: to last forever, to be everlasting
to make eternal, to make everlasting

examples.english: There will circulate among them young boys made eternal.

senses.gloss: to make ineffaceable, to make unforgettable
to remain, to stay, to abide, to linger
to grow very old, to enjoy a long life, to be long-lived
infinite duration, endless time, perpetuity, eternity

examples.text: مَا دَارَ فِي خَلَدِ الْأَيَّامِ لِي فَرَحٌ / أَبَا عُبَادَةَ حَتَّى دُرْتَ فِي خَلَدِي

etymology.text:
Compare Hebrew חלד (khéled, “world, era”) and Ge'ez ኀለደ (ḫälädä, “to last long”).

From Aramaic חוּלְדּא / ܚܘܠܕܐ (ḥuldā, “mole”). Compare Hebrew חולד (khóled).
